rulings.Case Title: ERIN WIDEN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss, And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs 2856-62 Washington Street Homeowners Association, Daniel P. Mulberry, Gaylen Mulderry, Peter Dubois, Leslie Zimmerman, Henrietta Hagopian, As Trustee Of The Hagopian Family Trust?S Complaint For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 20, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT STONE MELET, NICOLE MELET, DIVISADERO PLACE HOME OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, KATE KARDOS, STEVEN POLEVOI Notice Of Motion To Dismiss, And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs 2856-62 Washington Street Homeowners Association, Daniel P. Mulberry, Gaylen Mulderry, Peter Dubois, Leslie Zimmerman, Henrietta Hagopian, As Trustee Of The Hagopian Family Trust?S Complaint For Failure To Prosecute is GRANTED. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority on point in support of its argument that the cause of action for declaratory relief was automatically stayed due to a pending appeal in the consolidated matter or that it was "impossible, impractical or futile" to bring this matter to trial within 5 years. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY WEST VS. THE ANDREWS HOTEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANTS THE ANDREWS HOTEL AND PASCALE SCHMIDT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 20, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS THE ANDREWS HOTEL AND PASCALE SCHMIDT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 10, 2022. By no later than November 1, 2022 Plaintiff to file a supplemental declaration including a copy of the proposed amendments to the complaint including facts supporting Plaintiff's conclusory allegations, e.g. as to paragraph 24 facts supporting allegations that D was "aware of bedbug infestation in the hotel and, specifically, Plaintiff's room.;" as to paragraph 18 dates of on-line reviews and/or links to specific pages, whether D responded to any postings on the referenced sites, etc; whether Ds were notified in any other manner of the bed bug presence and dates of such notifications prior to Plaintiff's stay; as to paragraph 38 facts supporting the conclusion that "defendants deliberately chose to not eradicate the bedbug infestation." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PRESIDIO COLLINS PARTNERS, LLC, A LIMITED VS. YOULAND, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 20, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT PS FUNDING, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts support allegations re: duty to disclose and that the moving defendant "spoke on the subject with Plaintiff" (opposition 5:18). Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. rulings.Case Title: 1929 UNION, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. GARO OUSTAYAN, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Supporting Declarations Of Garo Oustayan And James A. Quadra Request For Judicial Notice Proposed Order Granting Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 20, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANT GARO OUSTAYAN, DIAMOND BROKERS OF LOS ALTOS, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration is GRANTED. Portion of the complaint pertaining to unit 1929 and the cross-complaint shall proceed in Arbitrations. Parties shall promptly select an arbitrator and are strongly encouraged to complete the arbitration before January 30, 2023 trial. Complaint regarding unit 1931 shall proceed in Court. The Court notes that Mr. Oustayan and his counsel acted inconsistently with an existence of an enforceable arbitration clause starting at least in January 2022. However, this behavior did not amount to waiver. The Court further notes that under existing authorities and the facts of this case the arbitration clause is enforceable. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MALDONADO E. SUSANA VS. LA VICTORIA BAKERY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Order Enforcing Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 20, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT JAIME MALDONADO Notice Of Motion To Vacate Order Enforcing Settlement is continued to November 18, 2022. Plaintiff to file a motion to Vacate the Dismissal and set it for hearing for November 18, 2022 per CCP 1005. Motion to Vacate Order Enforcing Settlement is also set for hearing for November 18, 2022 (original moving papers). Briefing for Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement shall be in accordance with CCP 1005 (opposition and reply). The Court will first hear the motion to Vacate Order Enforcing Settlement Agreement; if granted, the Court will hear the Motions to Vacate the Dismissal and to Enforce the Settlement Agreement; if denied, the Motions to Vacate and to Enforce will be taken off calendar as moot. To clarify the record, a properly executed substitution of attorney must be filed no later than October 20, 2022 by 5:00 p.m. listing Mr. Hadiaris as attorney of record. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
